_meta.title: SEALED — He Promised to Widen It. One Order Narrowed It. (Campus EO 13899, long-form)
_meta.purpose: One order, two ledgers. EO 13899 / IHRA / Title VI rendered as document-seal-timeline Remotion explainers over institutional backgrounds; unnamed editorial silhouettes for the donor + freshman; atmospheric campus/archive b-roll; BROKEN verdict owned by CR's OWN AUDIT (book separately logs Priority #3 Done). Zero Hedra/kling-i2v faces; zero Sora hyperreal faces; zero Wikipedia close-ups; 3rd-grade voter script.
_meta.verdict_ownership: BROKEN is Campaign Receipts' OWN AUDIT of the campus free-speech promise. The SEALED book separately logs the SAME order as a KEPT donor priority (A-I-P-A-C Priority #3, 'Done'). Never attribute BROKEN to the book.

props.text: People died so you could speak.

expected_on_screen_text: People died so you could speak.
clips.covers_script_section: NEW OPEN (gravitas) — cold open: 'People died so you could speak.' [pause:600ms]

props.text: 1776 — colonists fought a king for the right to speak against him. Some died for it.

clips.covers_script_section: NEW OPEN (gravitas) — 'In 1776, American colonists fought a war against a king... Some of them died for that.' [pause:600ms]

props.text: THE FIRST AMENDMENT. PAID FOR IN BLOOD.

clips.covers_script_section: NEW OPEN (gravitas) — 'The First Amendment. The right to speak... So when a line moves on what you can say, it matters. It was paid for in blood.' [pause:800ms]

props.text: One order moved that line. And nobody voted on it.

clips.covers_script_section: NEW OPEN (gravitas) — bridge into the order: 'On December 11, 2019, one order moved that line. And nobody voted on it.' [pause:400ms]

props.text: December 11, 2019 — one order kept a donor's promise and broke a voter's.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 1 — Hook line 1 (the dated receipt) — now 'That same order kept a donor's promise and broke a voter's.'

props.text: ONE PAPER. TWO LEDGERS.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 1 — 'One paper. Two ledgers.' big-word beat

clips.prompt: Slow push-in on a single signed executive order resting on a dark wooden desk in a quiet government office, warm desk-lamp light raking across the embossed seal at the top of the page. The pen lies beside it. Two faint paper ledgers are stacked just out of focus on either side. Investigative documentary realism, Frontline / Retro Report aesthetic. No people, no chyron, no flags.
clips.source_citation: EO 13899, signed Dec 11, 2019 (Federal Register) — framing visual for the master 'two ledgers' loop

props.text: You wanted campus free speech protected. That's a fair thing to want.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 2 — EMPATHY BEAT 1 / HOOK-ACK (opens the scene)

clips.prompt: Quiet establishing shot of an empty university lecture hall at golden hour, low sunlight through tall windows falling on rows of empty wooden seats, a closed campus handbook resting on the front desk. Calm, ordinary, hopeful. Documentary realism. No people, no text overlays, no banners.
clips.source_citation: Scene 2 establishing — 'the school your kid is about to pay for' (atmosphere, no claim)

props.quote: Restore free speech in America, and stop government censorship.
props.source: SEALED 2016 — promise as logged in the book

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 2 — the 2016 promise (book-line source card; VO stays paraphrased per no-quote lock)

props.quote: A non-legally binding working definition, with illustrative examples.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 3 — 'meet a definition. Keep your eye on it.' (the tracked object)

clips.prompt: Macro tracking shot drifting slowly across a printed document titled with a working definition, the page laid flat under soft museum lighting, fingers nowhere in frame. A magnifying glass rests at the corner. The paper looks ordinary, archival, harmless. Investigative still-life realism. No people, no logos, no readable headlines.
clips.source_citation: Scene 3 — 'just words on a page' (the guide before it gets teeth)

props.text: ON ITS OWN, A GUIDE FORCES NOTHING.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 3 — 'It was just words on a page. So how does a guide get teeth?' (open loop)

props.quote: Agencies shall consider the IHRA definition... a non-legally binding working definition.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 4 — the order signed; verbatim 'shall consider ... non-legally binding'

props.text: THAT'S THE DAY THE GUIDE STOPPED BEING JUST A GUIDE.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 4 — pays the Scene-3 loop ('how does a guide get teeth')

clips.prompt: Slow dolly across the top of a signed executive order on heavy paper, the embossed seal catching warm raking light, a fountain pen set down beside fresh ink. Behind it, an out-of-focus institutional office at dusk. The same document now reads heavier, official, weighted. Documentary realism, archival gravitas. No people, no flags, no chyron.
clips.source_citation: EO 13899 signed Dec 11, 2019 (Federal Register) — the order acquiring weight

props.citation: Title VI, Civil Rights Act of 1964
props.quote: No program receiving federal financial assistance may discriminate... on the ground of national origin.
props.source: The lever: it lets Washington pull a school's federal money

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 5 — 'Now meet the lever. It's called Title Six.'
Scene 5 — 'the guide's examples could feed a Title Six complaint' (guide → rule motif)

clips.prompt: A single anonymous college student, seen only from behind in silhouette, sits at a wooden desk in a sunlit classroom and quietly raises a hand to speak. Plain backpack, no logos, head turned away from camera, features unseen. Wide framing of the whole room, the student small in the frame. Stylized editorial illustration look, muted institutional palette. One ordinary student in an ordinary room. No crowd, no podium, no flags.
clips.source_citation: Scene 5 — 'here's a student. She says one sentence in class.' (unnamed editorial silhouette, the planted freshman)

props.text: AND NOBODY VOTED ON THAT.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 5 — 'the rule that sentence runs into just changed. And nobody voted on that.'

props.text: WHOSE PROMISE JUST GOT KEPT?

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 6 — EMPATHY BEAT 2 re-hook: 'Hold on. Whose promise just got kept here?'

clips.prompt: An anonymous first-year college student, shown from behind in soft silhouette, walks alone across a quiet campus quad in early autumn light carrying a backpack. Leaves on the path, old stone buildings out of focus. Head turned away, features unseen, framed at full distance. Stylized editorial illustration, calm and human, muted palette. One student arriving somewhere a line had already been drawn. No crowd, no banners, no flags.
clips.source_citation: Scene 6 — 'That student is a freshman. She just walked onto campus this fall.' (same unnamed silhouette, re-anchored)

props.text: It widened nothing for her. It moved where her words could go.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 6 — 'The order widened nothing for her.'

props.text: WATCH THE SAME MACHINE RUN TODAY.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 7 — late re-hook: 'This is not a 2019 history lesson.'
Scene 7 — dated timeline: Columbia July 2025 (IHRA) + 60 schools March 2025 (Title VI scale)

props.text: Columbia did it under a deal — to get the cut federal money back.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 7 — 'It did this under a deal. The deal restored federal money the government had cut.' (carries the deal sentence the timeline card skips)

props.label: CAMPUSES WARNED AT ONCE
props.caption: March 2025 — same Title VI lever (scale, not IHRA)

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 7 — '60 campuses got letters about the same Title Six lever'

props.text: Here's the part the headline skipped.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 8 — 'Here's the part the headline skipped.' (master-loop payoff opens)

clips.prompt: An anonymous lobbyist figure, seen only as a back-lit silhouette in a marble Washington corridor, closes a leather ledger and sets it on a desk. Body in shadow, head turned away, features unseen, not based on any real person. Wide framing down the corridor. Stylized editorial illustration, restrained institutional palette, cool light. Ordinary bookkeeping, a column entry being closed. No crowd, no flags, no campaign imagery.
clips.source_citation: Scene 8 — 'Every promise sits in some ledger somewhere. This one's a donor's.' (unnamed silhouette before the AIPAC name; EMPATHY FLAG 1 runway)

props.source: SEALED paper trail — the donor-side receipt (a KEPT donor priority)

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 8 — 'In that ledger, the order is Priority Three. The note reads Done.' (book = donor ledger, NOT the verdict)

props.text: ONE ACT. TWO LEDGERS.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 8 — 'So one act sits on two ledgers.' (pays the Scene-1 master loop)

clips.prompt: The same anonymous first-year student from earlier, shown from behind in soft silhouette, stands at the front of a quiet classroom and speaks one sentence, calm and unbothered. Head turned away, features unseen, framed across the whole room. Stylized editorial illustration, warm late-afternoon light, muted palette. She still says her sentence; the room around her is unchanged. No crowd, no podium, no flags.
clips.source_citation: Scene 9 — EMPATHY BEAT 3: 'Remember that freshman. She still says her sentence.' (same unnamed silhouette, closes the thread)

props.text: People fought and died for the right to speak.

clips.covers_script_section: NEW CLOSE (gravitas) — 'Here's what this order taught us. People fought and died for the right to speak.' [pause:400ms] (precedes the lesson line)

props.text: AND SHE NEVER GOT A VOTE.

expected_on_screen_text: AND SHE NEVER GOT A VOTE
clips.covers_script_section: Scene 9 — 'Remember that freshman. She still says her sentence. The line just sits somewhere new now. And she never got a vote.' [pause:600ms]

props.text: That right is only as wide as the rule behind it.

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 9 — the lesson line, now reframed: 'That right is only as wide as the rule behind it.'

props.text: NO WAR. NO VOTE. JUST A SIGNATURE.

clips.covers_script_section: NEW CLOSE (gravitas) — 'One order, signed by one person, can quietly move that line. No war. No vote. Just a signature.' [pause:600ms]

props.text: PROTECT IT FOR WHOM? WHERE IS THE LINE?

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 9 — 'So next time someone says protect free speech, ask two things. Protect it for whom. And where is the line.'

props.promise: Protect campus free speech (widen the debate)

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 9 — 'By our own audit, that promise is BROKEN.' (CR's OWN AUDIT verdict, NOT the book)

props.text: WOULD YOU COUNT IT?

clips.covers_script_section: Scene 9 — comment trigger: 'Would you count that promise as kept?'

props.text: PEOPLE DIED TO WIDEN THAT LINE.

expected_on_screen_text: PEOPLE DIED TO WIDEN THAT LINE
clips.covers_script_section: Scene 9 — 'He promised to protect campus speech. Then one order drew the line around one kind of speech. People died to widen that line.' (carries the close before the comment-trigger question)
Scene 9 — CTA cold close ('The full receipt and free newsletter are linked below.')
